csharp.note: 2 failures are pre-existing DLL fixture issue

uncovered_files: PLang/Runtime2/modules/http/types.cs (0% — TransferProgress record, no direct test needed)

v3_revised_findings_status.finding_1_autodetect_false_green: FIXED — now verifies body == 'auto content' (file data, not filename)
v3_revised_findings_status.finding_2_json_parsing_false_green: FIXED — now verifies serialized value contains 'Alice'
v3_revised_findings_status.finding_3_upload_response_false_green: FIXED — now verifies Value not null + JSON contains '42'
v3_revised_findings_status.finding_4_form_upload_0_percent: FIXED — 4 tests: dict auto-detect, explicit form, @file reference with content verification, object→JSON
v3_revised_findings_status.finding_6_binary_bytes: FIXED — now checks bytes match input

findings.issue: StreamWithProgressAsync at 44.4% coverage — progress callback reporting during download/upload is only partially tested. The throttled reporting (500ms interval) and TransferProgress record construction have gaps.
findings.impact: Low — progress is a UX feature, not a correctness feature. Partial coverage is acceptable.
findings.suggestion: Non-blocking. Could add a download test with Content-Length header to exercise percentage calculation.

findings.issue: TryExtractSignedErrorIdentity at 0% — best-effort extraction of signed identity from error responses. Only called on error path when response might contain a signed body.
findings.impact: Low — this is a best-effort error enrichment path. If it fails, the error is still returned correctly.
findings.suggestion: Non-blocking. Would need a signed error response body to test, which requires full signing setup.

findings.issue: Get_PlangResponseInvalidSignature_ReturnsError (L866) — only checks result.Success is false, no Error.Key check. Acceptable because error type depends on Ed25519 verification behavior which may vary.
findings.impact: Very low — the test correctly verifies that invalid signatures cause failure.

summary: All previous findings resolved. DefaultHttpProvider at 95.9% line coverage (up from 5.7% in v2). 59 HTTP tests covering: response parsing (JSON/XML/text/binary/plang), URL resolution (relative/absolute/protocol prefix), error handling (404/500/timeout/NoBaseUrl/ConfigLocked/UnsignedPlang), exception mapping (HttpRequestException/IOException/FormatException), streaming (Lines/SSE/SSE-multiline/Bytes/Plang-signed/Plang-invalid/error/custom-var/unsigned-rejection), signing integration (X-Signature header/JSON format/plang response verification/invalid signature), header merging (default+step/content routing), upload (text/file/base64/form/@file/auto-detect-file/auto-detect-string/auto-detect-object), download (save/exists-error/exists-skip/exists-overwrite/parent-dirs/404), and config scope chain (timeout/baseUrl/default-true/default-false/redirect-lock/null-skip/per-step-override). 3 minor findings remain, all non-blocking. Test suite is honest and thorough.
